segments.text: Now Jehoshaphat had riches and honor in abundance, and he allied himself with Ahab by marriage.
And some years later he went down to visit Ahab in Samaria, where Ahab sacrificed many sheep and cattle for him and the people with him, and urged him to march up to Ramoth-gilead.
Ahab king of Israel asked Jehoshaphat king of Judah,
Will you go with me against Ramoth-gilead?
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: And Jehoshaphat replied,
I am like you, and my people are your people; we will join you in the war.
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: But Jehoshaphat also said to the king of Israel,
Please inquire first for the word of the LORD.
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: So the king of Israel assembled the prophets, four hundred men, and asked them,
Should we go to war against Ramoth-gilead, or should we refrain?
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: Go up,
segments.speaker: Prophets

segments.text: they replied,
and God will deliver it into the hand of the king.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But Jehoshaphat asked,
Is there not still a prophet of the LORD here of whom we can inquire?
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: The king of Israel answered,
There is still one man who can ask the LORD, but I hate him because he never prophesies anything good for me, but only bad. He is Micaiah son of Imlah.
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: The king should not say that!
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: Jehoshaphat replied.
So the king of Israel called one of his officials and said,
Bring Micaiah son of Imlah at once.
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: Dressed in royal attire, the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat king of Judah were sitting on their thrones at the threshing floor by the entrance of the gate of Samaria, with all the prophets prophesying before them.
Now Zedekiah son of Chenaanah had made for himself iron horns and declared,
This is what the LORD says: ‘With these you shall gore the Arameans until they are finished off.’
segments.speaker: Zedekiah

segments.text: And all the prophets were prophesying the same, saying,
Go up to Ramoth-gilead and prosper, for the LORD will deliver it into the hand of the king.
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: Then the messenger who had gone to call Micaiah instructed him,
Behold, with one accord the words of the prophets are favorable to the king. So please let your words be like theirs, and speak favorably.
segments.speaker: Messenger

segments.text: But Micaiah said,
As surely as the LORD lives, I will speak whatever my God tells me.
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: When Micaiah arrived, the king asked him,
Micaiah, should we go to war against Ramoth-gilead, or should we refrain?
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: Go up and triumph,
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Micaiah replied,
for they will be given into your hand.
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: But the king said to him,
How many times must I make you swear not to tell me anything but the truth in the name of the LORD?
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: So Micaiah declared:
I saw all Israel scattered on the hillslike sheep without a shepherd. And the LORD said, ‘These people have no master; let each one return home in peace.’
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Then the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat,
Did I not tell you that he never prophesies good for me, but only bad?
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Micaiah continued,
Therefore hear the word of the LORD: I saw the LORD sitting on His throne, and all the host of heaven standing on His right and on His left.
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text:
And the LORD said, ‘Who will entice Ahab king of Israel to march up and fall at Ramoth-gilead?’
And one suggested this, and another that.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
Then a spirit came forward, stood before the LORD, and said, ‘I will entice him.’
‘By what means?’ asked the LORD.
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text:
And he replied, ‘I will go out and be a lying spirit in the mouths of all his prophets.’
‘You will surely entice him and prevail,’ said the LORD. ‘Go and do it.’
segments.speaker: Spirit

segments.text: So you see, the LORD has put a lying spirit in the mouths of these prophets of yours, and the LORD has pronounced disaster against you.
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Then Zedekiah son of Chenaanah went up, struck Micaiah in the face, and demanded,
Which way did the Spirit of the LORD go when He departed from me to speak with you?
segments.speaker: Zedekiah

segments.text: Micaiah replied,
You will soon see, on that day when you go and hide in an inner room.
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: And the king of Israel declared,
Take Micaiah and return him to Amon the governor of the city and to Joash the king’s son,
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: and tell them that this is what the king says: ‘Put this man in prison and feed him only bread and water until I return safely.’
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: But Micaiah replied,
If you ever return safely, the LORD has not spoken through me.
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Then he added,
Take heed, all you people!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat king of Judah went up to Ramoth-gilead.
And the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat,
I will disguise myself and go into battle, but you wear your royal robes.
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: So the king of Israel disguised himself and went into battle.
Now the king of Aram had ordered his chariot commanders,
Do not fight with anyone, small or great, except the king of Israel.
segments.speaker: King of Syria

segments.text: When the chariot commanders saw Jehoshaphat, they said,
This is the king of Israel!
segments.speaker: Chariot Captains

segments.text: So they turned to fight against him, but Jehoshaphat cried out, and the LORD helped him. God drew them away from him.
And when the chariot commanders saw that he was not the king of Israel, they turned back from pursuing him.
However, a certain man drew his bow without taking special aim, and he struck the king of Israel between the joints of his armor. So the king said to his charioteer,
Turn around and take me out of the battle, for I am badly wounded!
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: The battle raged throughout that day, and the king of Israel propped himself up in his chariot facing the Arameans until evening. And at sunset he died.
